 {"id":"asupersync-1kneh","title":"[AA][Cancel] Obligation ledger (linear tokens)","description":"## Goal
Implement the obligation ledger as a linear‑token registry with strict commit/abort rules.

## Why
Obligations are the runtime’s safety currency. Leaks must be impossible by construction.

## Deliverables
- Registry with acquire/commit/abort APIs.
- Linear token types that enforce resolution.
- Leak oracle that fails tests deterministically.

## Acceptance
- Every permit/ack/lease is resolved or the test fails.
- Static + dynamic checks catch all obligation leaks.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:06:41.181468640Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T02:54:19.978593638Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T02:54:19.978508150Z","close_reason":"Implemented ObligationLedger with linear tokens, acquire/commit/abort APIs, per-region/per-task queries, deterministic leak oracle, and 15 passing tests","external_ref":"bd-35ju","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"asupersync-1kqaw","title":"[AA][Cap] Ambient authority audit + hardening","description":"## Goal
Audit the entire codebase for ambient authority (implicit IO/time/random/spawn).

## Why
Hidden effects break determinism and security. Every effect must flow through explicit capabilities.

## Deliverables
- Audit checklist + grep patterns.
- Fixes for any ambient effects.
- Regression tests to prevent reintroduction.

## Acceptance
- No ambient authority remains in core runtime.
- New violations are caught by CI.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:09:29.456817845Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:59:44.582959700Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T03:59:44.582885202Z","close_reason":"Audit complete in src/audit/ambient.rs with KNOWN_FINDINGS array + CI grep patterns. Found 8 non-exempt violations (3 time, 4 spawn, 2 entropy) and 5 exempt (by design). Added 2 missing spawn violations (server/connection.rs, http/h1/stream.rs). All effects in core runtime flow through Cx capabilities. Regression prevention via GREP_PATTERNS const for CI.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1kqaw","depends_on_id":"asupersync-20kbt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1kqaw","depends_on_id":"asupersync-22uy7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1ksog","title":"HTTP/3 E2E test suite: QUIC transport and HTTP/3 protocol tests","description":"# HTTP/3 E2E Test Suite

## Overview
Comprehensive end-to-end test suite for HTTP/3 implementation covering
QUIC transport, HTTP/3 protocol, and cancel-correctness.

## Test Directory Structure
```
tests/e2e/h3/
├── mod.rs                       # Test module root
├── common/
│   ├── mod.rs                   # Shared utilities
│   ├── certs.rs                 # Test certificates
│   └── fixtures.rs              # Test data
├── quic/
│   ├── endpoint.rs              # Endpoint tests
│   ├── connection.rs            # Connection tests
│   ├── streams.rs               # Stream tests
│   └── cancel.rs                # Cancellation tests
├── h3/
│   ├── client.rs                # Client tests
│   ├── server.rs                # Server tests
│   ├── headers.rs               # Header handling
│   ├── body.rs                  # Body streaming
│   └── cancel.rs                # Cancellation tests
├── interop/
│   ├── curl.rs                  # Interop with curl
│   ├── browser.rs               # Browser compatibility
│   └── quic_go.rs               # Go QUIC server
└── lab/
    ├── deterministic.rs         # Lab runtime tests
    └── loss_simulation.rs       # Packet loss tests
```

## Test Categories

### 1. QUIC Transport Tests
- Endpoint creation and binding
- Connection establishment
- 0-RTT resumption
- Stream creation and data transfer
- Connection migration (optional)

### 2. HTTP/3 Protocol Tests
- Request/response round-trip
- Multiple concurrent requests
- Header compression (QPACK)
- Body streaming
- Trailers

### 3. Cancel-Correctness Tests
- Cancel during QUIC handshake
- Cancel during request
- Cancel during response body
- Server shutdown with active requests
- Connection close with pending streams

### 4. Interoperability Tests
- curl --http3 compatibility
- Browser compatibility (Chrome, Firefox)
- Go quic-go server

### 5. Lab Runtime Tests
- Deterministic packet ordering
- Simulated packet loss
- Simulated latency

## Logging Requirements
- TRACE: Packet-level details
- DEBUG: Stream/request flow
- INFO: Test progress
- WARN: Protocol anomalies
- ERROR: Test failures

## Test Scripts

### Run All HTTP/3 Tests
```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
export RUST_LOG="asupersync=debug,test=info"

echo "=== HTTP/3 E2E Tests ==="
cargo test -p asupersync --test e2e_h3 -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture 2>&1 | tee h3_tests.log
```

### Run Interop Tests
```bash
# Requires curl with HTTP/3 support
cargo test -p asupersync --test e2e_h3 interop:: -- --nocapture
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All QUIC transport tests pass
- [ ] All HTTP/3 protocol tests pass
- [ ] Cancel-correctness verified
- [ ] Interop tests with curl/browsers
- [ ] Lab runtime tests deterministic
- [ ] Structured logging
- [ ] CI integration","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T05:23:41.851091448Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T06:50:08.920501867Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T06:50:08.920421828Z","close_reason":"done","external_ref":"bd-ifn2","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","ecosystem-parity","http","quic"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1ksog","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1gfmo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:09Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"asupersync-1ky0b","title":"[AA][Formal] Cancellation protocol formalization","description":"## Goal
Formalize the cancellation protocol as a state machine with budgets and phase transitions.

## Why
Cancellation is a multi‑phase protocol (request→drain→finalize). We need proof‑level clarity.

## Deliverables
- Formal state machine spec with guards and budgets.
- Proof sketch of idempotence and bounded cleanup.
- Mapping from runtime transitions to spec.

## Acceptance
- Spec covers CancelRequested/Cancelling/Finalizing/Completed.
- Proves that all tasks eventually reach terminal outcomes under sufficient budgets.

## Dependencies
- Uses the core small‑step semantics.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:05:36.612171824Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T02:25:39.817956116Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T02:25:39.817887909Z","close_reason":"Formalized cancellation protocol with budgets, idempotence, bounded cleanup","external_ref":"bd-1b5t","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
